pi_items.source: Verified via xarray on real NOAA NCEP Reanalysis datasets. The datasets span 0-360 degrees East, so 90W mathematically converts to 270E.
Verified via xarray. The air temperature dataset contains an exact 0.0 latitude coordinate, and Panoply correctly snaps to the closest point when 0 is requested.

Verified via xarray data extraction at (0, 270). The maximum monthly air temperature (~25.95) and precipitation rate (~3.069e-05) both definitively peak in index 2 (March), closely followed by index 1 (February), confirming the valid target months logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world NCEP Reanalysis climate data obtained from NOAA. Independent verification using python xarray confirms that the datasets (air.mon.ltm.nc and prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc) utilize a 0-360 East longitude grid format. Therefore, extracting data for the Galapagos Islands at 90°W correctly requires converting the target longitude to 270°E, as explicitly claimed by the task metadata. Furthermore, at these exact coordinates (0° Lat, 270° Lon), both the maximum air temperature (~25.95) and precipitation rate (~3.069e-05) empirically peak in March, closely followed by February, which fully validates the expected valid peak months used in the verifier's scoring logic.
